question: What is the definition of the abbreviation PZR?

nuscale_ch01_0019.title: Operating Characteristics
nuscale_ch01_0019.summary: The NuScale Power Module (NPM) operates using natural circulation for reactor coolant flow, eliminating the need for reactor coolant pumps, and features a small, modular, integral pressurized water reactor design. It employs passive safety systems—including dedicated emergency core cooling and decay heat removal systems—that do not rely on electrical power, and its components are designed for in-shop fabrication and enhanced safety through features like a vacuum-operated steel containment partially immersed in water. The NPM allows for flexible power maneuvering using control rods and soluble boron, with all major components integrated within the reactor pressure vessel.

nuscale_ch01_0117.title: Figure 1.7-3f: Piping and In strumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0117.summary: This section provides the legend for piping and instrumentation diagrams (P&IDs) used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report. It lists and describes various symbols representing sample probes, pressure and temperature sensors, flow measurement devices, and other instrumentation and accessory devices used in the diagrams. These legends ensure consistent interpretation of the technical drawings throughout the report.

nuscale_ch01_0769.title: SRP 15.6.1, Rev 2: Inadvertent Opening of a PWR Pressurizer Relief Valve or a BWR Pressure Relief ValveAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0769.summary: The NuScale design partially conforms to SRP 15.6.1, which addresses the inadvertent opening of pressurizer relief valves, because NuScale does not use power-operated relief valves (PORVs) that are susceptible to inadvertent operation. Instead, NuScale uses spring-loaded ASME code safety relief valves, and any mechanical failure of these valves is considered within the analysis of inadvertent ECCS valve actuation in another section. Thus, the guidance is only partially applicable to the NuScale design.

nuscale_ch01_0111.title: Figure 1.7-2: Instrumentatio n and Controls Symbol Legend
nuscale_ch01_0111.summary: This section provides a legend explaining the symbols used for instrumentation and controls in NuScale’s safety analysis report diagrams. It defines the logic gate functions, control room indicators, switch types, and other symbols relevant to system status and operations, ensuring clear interpretation of the diagrams. Additionally, it includes notes on piping and instrumentation diagram conventions, such as vent and drain representations.

nuscale_ch01_0013.title: Proprietary Information
nuscale_ch01_0013.summary: This section explains the organization and identification of text, tables, figures, and page numbering within the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR). It also clarifies that while the FSAR does not contain proprietary or safeguards information, some sensitive content is withheld from public disclosure in accordance with regulations, and a separate public version is provided with such material removed.

nuscale_ch01_0014.title: Acronyms and Abbreviations
nuscale_ch01_0014.summary: This section explains the conventions used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) for numbering tables, figures, and pages, as well as the handling of proprietary and sensitive information. Tables and figures are numbered according to their section, and sensitive content is withheld from the public version in accordance with regulatory requirements.

edges.description: Section B provides diagram legends that may define abbreviations such as PZR, which could be referenced or used in Section A's discussion of operating characteristics.
Section B mentions 'PWR Pressurizer Relief Valve,' which implies the use of the abbreviation 'PZR' (Pressurizer), thereby providing the context needed to define 'PZR' as used in Section A.

Section B references the term 'pressurizer' (PZR) in the context of pressurizer relief valves, while Section A provides diagram legends where the abbreviation PZR would be defined, thus Section A specifies the meaning of the abbreviation used in Section B.

Section A provides general information about the structure and referencing of figures and tables in the FSAR, while Section B contains a specific figure (Figure 1.7-3f) that may include the definition or legend for the abbreviation PZR, connecting them through the documentation structure.

Section A provides general information about acronyms and abbreviations in the FSAR, while Section B contains a figure with legends that may include the abbreviation 'PZR', connecting them through the use and explanation of abbreviations in technical documentation.
Section B is likely where the definition of the abbreviation PZR would be found, as it pertains to acronyms and abbreviations, while Section A does not address abbreviations.
